title: As If A Phantom Caress'd Me
content:
As if a phantom caress'd me,
I thought I was not alone, walking here by the shore;
But the one I thought was with me, as now I walk by the shore--the one I loved, that caress'd me,
As I lean and look through the glimmering light--that one has utterly disappear'd,
And those appear that are hateful to me, and mock me.
meaning:
Walt Whitman's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "As If A Phantom Caress'd Me"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
